methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Mujin is a Series C robotics and manufacturing automation company that has raised $85M+ to date. Valuation analysis relies on two methods applied against a peer set of nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies comprising eight private firms and one public comparable (277810.KQ). Key private peers include 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C).

Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.7x multiplier to the $85M raised proxy, yielding $144M. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company comp universe produces $1.6B, representing the midpoint of a $300M to $10.0B range. The wide dispersion reflects maturity and market positioning variance within the robotics sector at this stage.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $123M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $872M. Key uncertainties include (1) revenue traction and unit economics relative to more mature peers like Exotec, (2) technology differentiation versus the broader cohort, and (3) path to Series D funding and subsequent exit multiples, given the 1.7x stage-step assumption embedded in the lower bound. The peer-median approach carries greater weight given direct comparability but reflects the sector's wide valuation dispersion.
